description: ol-undefined-var (issue #113): a postfixed Place's base is always checked as a read, whether the Place is used as a value (`print :missing.field`, line 1) or as a segmented assignment target (`:absent.count = 1`, line 2) — spec/execution-model.md:251-291 is explicit that there is no auto-vivification, so a segmented target's base must already be a bound variable. Both bases here are never declared, so each raises `ol-undefined-var` at the base's own span. This locks in the fix for a genuine bug in the rule's first draft, which never checked a Place base as a read at all.
